args:
set -eu
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cat >"$TMPDIR/openssl.cnf" <<'EOF'
[req]
default_bits = 2048
prompt = no
default_md = sha256
distinguished_name = dn
x509_extensions = v3_req

[dn]
CN = localhost

[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
basicConstraints = critical,CA:FALSE
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF
openssl req -x509 -nodes -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout "$TMPDIR/key.pem" -out "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" -days 1 -config "$TMPDIR/openssl.cnf" >/dev/null 2>&1
cargo run --quiet -- serve-peer-quic --bind 127.0.0.1:9443 --cert "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" --key "$TMPDIR/key.pem" >"$TMPDIR/server.log" 2>&1 &
SERVER_PID=$!
trap 'kill "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT
sleep 2
cargo run --quiet -- peer-ping-quic --addr 127.0.0.1:9443 --server-name localhost --ca-cert "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" >"$TMPDIR/ping.json"
TMPDIR_PATH="$TMPDIR" python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, pathlib
payload = json.loads(pathlib.Path(os.environ["TMPDIR_PATH"], "ping.json").read_text())
assert payload["type"] == "handshake_accepted", payload
assert payload["protocol_version"] == "0.1.0", payload
PY
cargo run --quiet -- peer-execute-quic --addr 127.0.0.1:9443 --server-name localhost --ca-cert "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" --prompt 'fozzy quic execute' >"$TMPDIR/execute.json"
TMPDIR_PATH="$TMPDIR" python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, pathlib
payload = json.loads(pathlib.Path(os.environ["TMPDIR_PATH"], "execute.json").read_text())
assert payload["type"] == "execute_job", payload
assert payload["record"]["status"] in {"completed", "recovered"}, payload
assert payload["record"]["result"]["backend"] == "mlx", payload
PY

set -eu
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cat >"$TMPDIR/openssl.cnf" <<'EOF'
[req]
default_bits = 2048
prompt = no
default_md = sha256
distinguished_name = dn
x509_extensions = v3_req

[dn]
CN = localhost

[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
basicConstraints = critical,CA:FALSE
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF
openssl req -x509 -nodes -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout "$TMPDIR/key.pem" -out "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" -days 1 -config "$TMPDIR/openssl.cnf" >/dev/null 2>&1
cargo run --quiet -- serve-peer-quic --bind 127.0.0.1:9443 --cert "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" --key "$TMPDIR/key.pem" >"$TMPDIR/server.log" 2>&1 &
SERVER_PID=$!
trap 'kill "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT
sleep 2
cargo run --quiet -- peer-ping-quic --addr 127.0.0.1:9443 --server-name localhost --ca-cert "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" >"$TMPDIR/ping.json"
TMPDIR_PATH="$TMPDIR" python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, pathlib
payload = json.loads(pathlib.Path(os.environ["TMPDIR_PATH"], "ping.json").read_text())
assert payload["type"] == "handshake_accepted", payload
assert payload["protocol_version"] == "0.1.0", payload
PY
cargo run --quiet -- peer-execute-quic --addr 127.0.0.1:9443 --server-name localhost --ca-cert "$TMPDIR/cert.pem" --prompt 'fozzy quic execute' >"$TMPDIR/execute.json"
TMPDIR_PATH="$TMPDIR" python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, pathlib
payload = json.loads(pathlib.Path(os.environ["TMPDIR_PATH"], "execute.json").read_text())
assert payload["type"] == "execute_job", payload
assert payload["record"]["status"] in {"completed", "recovered"}, payload
assert payload["record"]["result"]["backend"] == "mlx", payload
PY
